frameworks.name: brainstem arousal systems
frameworks.one_line: neuromodulatory arousal centers drive flexible sensorimotor mapping

frameworks.name: cortical correlated variability
frameworks.one_line: shared fluctuations between stimulus and action population codes index flexibility

frameworks.name: computational latent-variable model
frameworks.one_line: model-inferred latent states track hidden rule switches and pupil

core_question: Do brainstem arousal systems mediate flexible stimulus-response mapping by reorganizing cortical correlations between sensory and motor codes?
key_assumptions: pupil size indexes brainstem arousal state
correlated variability between cortical population codes reflects task-relevant routing
hidden rule changes can be tracked by a latent-variable inference model
manipulations: hidden, unpredictable changes in the stimulus-response mapping rule
orientation reports via button press across rule regimes
key_findings: rule switches evoked brainstem fMRI and pupil-dilation responses
model-inferred latent variables tracked rule-switching behavior and pupil
brainstem activity and pupil dilation preceded increases in cortical stimulus-action code correlations
interpretations: brainstem arousal systems are implicated in flexible input-output mapping
arousal-linked signals promote reorganization of sensorimotor cortical pathways for adaptive decisions
